coreCompetencies: Basic quality dimensions: completeness, accuracy, consistency, timeliness
Profiles a dataset to spot nulls, duplicates and outliers
Runs simple validation checks on a table
Documents data quality issues with clear reproduction steps
Notions of data cleansing and standardization
Reads existing data tests and interprets their results
Basic SQL to inspect data and count anomalies
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with a data quality tool
indicators: Needs 6-12 months of mentoring in data quality

coreCompetencies: Writes quality rules for completeness, uniqueness and value ranges
Implements automated tests with dbt tests or Great Expectations
Profiles datasets and documents expected value distributions
Sets up freshness and volume checks on pipelines
Detects anomalies and raises alerts on failed checks
Triages data quality incidents and documents findings
Produces data quality reports and dashboards
complementaryCompetencies: Hands-on with Great Expectations or dbt tests
indicators: Detects anomalies and maintains quality metrics

coreCompetencies: Designs data quality test suites across a domain's pipelines
Builds automated quality gates in the pipeline (dbt tests, Great Expectations)
Profiles data and defines expectations for critical datasets
Implements freshness, completeness and consistency monitoring with alerts
Sets up data quarantine for records that fail validation
Runs root cause analysis on data quality incidents
Reports quality SLAs and communicates with stakeholders
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with a data catalog and lineage for impact analysis
indicators: Leads quality work for a domain

coreCompetencies: Architects the data quality platform for multiple domains
Defines the enterprise quality framework and rule catalog
Implements quality gates, quarantine and remediation at scale
Builds freshness, completeness and consistency monitoring with SLAs
Introduces anomaly detection for quality signals
Leads root cause analysis and prevention for recurring incidents
Drives a data quality culture and cost-benefit prioritization
complementaryCompetencies: DataOps and continuous integration for quality
indicators: Defines quality strategy for the organization

coreCompetencies: Leads data quality engineering across domains
Defines quality standards, rules and ownership
Governs quality SLAs for freshness, completeness and consistency
Scales quarantine, remediation and incident response processes
Sets up observability with anomaly detection and alerting
Establishes root cause analysis practices and prevention
Measures quality ROI and prioritizes the roadmap
complementaryCompetencies: Quality in a data mesh operating model

coreCompetencies: Directs the data quality strategy for a business unit
Manages quality programs and incident response at scale
Defines quality architecture: rules engine, monitoring, quarantine
Leads global quality SLAs across regions and pipelines
Establishes anomaly detection and observability standards
Governs root cause analysis, prevention and postmortems
Reports quality KPIs and reliability to leadership
complementaryCompetencies: Quality in mergers and platform integrations

coreCompetencies: Sets the multi-year data quality strategy for the company
Establishes the quality operating model and reliability standards
Standardizes quality gates, SLAs and quarantine enterprise-wide
Governs freshness, completeness and consistency monitoring at scale
Defines anomaly detection and observability across all pipelines
Institutionalizes root cause analysis and incident prevention
Advises executives on data trust and reliability
complementaryCompetencies: Quality for regulated data and reporting
indicators: Reports to the CDO on data reliability
Recognized internal authority on data quality

coreCompetencies: Owns the enterprise data quality vision across all domains
Aligns quality with business reliability and risk
Defines reference quality models adopted company-wide
Leads quality for mergers, acquisitions and integrations
Establishes SLA, monitoring and quarantine standards at scale
Sets anomaly detection and observability frameworks
Sponsors reliability engineering and quality communities
complementaryCompetencies: Shapes external tooling and standards relationships
Advises the board on data reliability and trust
indicators: Sits on the data leadership committee

coreCompetencies: Sets and executes the enterprise data quality and reliability vision
Aligns quality with corporate strategy and risk posture
Owns the data trust and reliability framework across the company
Builds and leads the quality engineering organization
Defines the quality operating model, SLAs and observability
Establishes incident response and root cause practices globally
Governs quarantine, remediation and prevention at scale
complementaryCompetencies: Influences data reliability standards in the industry
